steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice most effectively begins a narrative about Mara Oberon's life. A narrative opening should establish the chronological and biographical starting point — who, where, and when the story begins — so the rest of the narrative can unfold from there.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Mara Oberon's story begins in Mumbai (then known as Bombay), India, in 1911.’: Uses explicit narrative framing ("story begins") and anchors all three grounding elements: who (Mara Oberon), where (Mumbai, India), and when (1911, her birth year). This is the natural starting point of a life narrative. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘Mara Oberon appeared in many films, including *The Three Passions* (1928) and *Interval* (1973), and was the first Indian-born actress to be nominated for an Academy Award.’: Jumps immediately into career highlights without establishing who she is or where her story begins. This reads as a career summary, not a narrative opening. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘In 1973, Mara Oberon played the role of Serena Moore in the film *Interval*.’: Starts at the end of her career (1973), skipping over her entire early life and origins. Opening a life narrative in 1973 abandons the beginning of the story. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Though she would go on to receive credit in many films, Mara Oberon also played nameless, uncredited roles in many films, such as in the movie *The Three Passions* (1928).’: Leads with a future contrast ("would go on") before establishing who Oberon is or when and where her story starts. This is a mid-career observation, not a biographical opening. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
